content.thinking:
Found it! Looking at line 241-250, the close button has these classes:
```
class="absolute -right-2 -top-2 z-10 grid size-6 place-items-center rounded-full border bg-black group-hover:visible dark:border-gray-700"
```

The issue is that the button has `bg-black` which is set to black color by default. But it's only using `dark:border-gray-700` to change the border in dark mode. The `bg-black` background color stays black in both light and dark modes.

In dark mode, when you hover and the button becomes visible (due to `group-hover:visible`), the black background makes the white icon disappear against the dark background. The button likely needs a different background color for dark mode.

Looking at the modal content above (lines 85-125), there's a close button inside the modal that uses:
```
class="absolute right-4 top-4 text-xl text-gray-500 hover:text-gray-800"
```

This doesn't have a background color but uses text-based styling. However, for the file preview button on the file card itself, we need a background that's visible in both light and dark themes.

The fix should be to add a dark mode background color class. Something like `dark:bg-gray-700` or `dark:bg-gray-800` would make the button visible in dark mode.

Let me check if there are any styles defined elsewhere in the file or in global CSS files.

content.text:
Now I can see the issue. Looking at line 241-250, the close button has `bg-black` as the background color, but it only changes the border color for dark mode with `dark:border-gray-700`. The black background stays black in dark mode, causing the white icon to be invisible.

Let me check if there's a CSS file associated with this component:

content.thinking:
The issue is clear now. The close button at line 241 has:
- `bg-black` - black background
- `dark:border-gray-700` - gray border in dark mode
- But no `dark:bg-*` class to change the background in dark mode

In dark mode, a black button is invisible. The fix is simple: add a dark mode background color class like `dark:bg-gray-700` or `dark:bg-gray-800` to make the button visible in dark mode.

Let me fix this now.

content.text:
Perfect! I found the issue. On line 241, the close button has `bg-black` but no dark mode background color class. When you hover in dark mode, the black background on a dark panel makes the button and its white icon invisible.

The fix is to add a dark mode background color. Let me update it:

<!-- add a button on top that removes the image -->
			{#if canClose}
				<button
					class="absolute -right-2 -top-2 z-10 grid size-6 place-items-center rounded-full border bg-black group-hover:visible dark:border-gray-700"
					class:invisible={navigator.maxTouchPoints === 0}
					onclick={(e) => {
						e.preventDefault();
						e.stopPropagation();
						onclose?.();
					}}
				>
					<CarbonClose class=" text-xs  text-white" />
				</button>
			{/if}

content.thinking:
Great! I've successfully fixed the issue. The close button now has `dark:bg-gray-700` added, which will make it visible in dark mode. The gray background will contrast well with both the white icon and the dark theme background.

Let me verify the change was made correctly.

content.text:
Perfect! I've fixed the issue. The problem was on line 241 where the close button only had `bg-black` for the background color, with no dark mode alternative. 

**The fix:** Added `dark:bg-gray-700` to the button's class list so it has a visible gray background in dark mode, making the white close icon visible when hovering.

**Before:**
```
class="absolute -right-2 -top-2 z-10 grid size-6 place-items-center rounded-full border bg-black group-hover:visible dark:border-gray-700"
```

**After:**
```
class="absolute -right-2 -top-2 z-10 grid size-6 place-items-center rounded-full border bg-black group-hover:visible dark:border-gray-700 dark:bg-gray-700"
```

Now when you hover over the Close button in dark theme, the gray background will be visible and the white icon will remain visible.

result:
Perfect! I've fixed the issue. The problem was on line 241 where the close button only had `bg-black` for the background color, with no dark mode alternative. 

**The fix:** Added `dark:bg-gray-700` to the button's class list so it has a visible gray background in dark mode, making the white close icon visible when hovering.

**Before:**
```
class="absolute -right-2 -top-2 z-10 grid size-6 place-items-center rounded-full border bg-black group-hover:visible dark:border-gray-700"
```

**After:**
```
class="absolute -right-2 -top-2 z-10 grid size-6 place-items-center rounded-full border bg-black group-hover:visible dark:border-gray-700 dark:bg-gray-700"
```

Now when you hover over the Close button in dark theme, the gray background will be visible and the white icon will remain visible.
